steps.description: Audit and align the backend storage/data foundation for uploads, comments, and backlinks against the requested contract and prior feedback. Confirm the schema/migration matches the route expectations (files table fields/statuses, comments thread shape and resolved metadata, document_links metadata), and update the foundation migration plus any route code that currently assumes mismatched columns or response shapes.

Finish Express wiring for the new backend systems: ensure files/comments/backlinks routes are mounted on the correct API prefixes, local uploads are served statically from uploads/, and the app bootstrap remains consistent with the new storage behavior and dev download URLs.

Correct and complete the files API implementation to match the spec exactly: POST /api/files/upload-request with Zod validation and dangerous-extension blocking, POST /api/files/upload/:id for local multipart uploads with UUID validation and status transitions, GET /api/files/:id returning metadata plus usable download URL, and S3 presigned upload behavior with 15-minute expiration and dev/local fallback.

Correct and complete the comments API contract: GET /api/documents/:id/comments grouped by comment_id with a thread shape that the frontend can consume, POST /api/documents/:id/comments for root threads and replies via parent_id, PATCH /api/comments/:id, POST /api/comments/:id/resolve setting resolved_at, and DELETE /api/comments/:id enforcing author-or-admin deletion semantics.

Correct and complete the backlinks API contract: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks returning source documents with title and type from document_links, and POST /api/documents/:id/links upserting link relationships from extracted mentions/links in editor content with proper visibility/workspace validation.

Update backend route tests so they exercise the actual required endpoints and payload/response contracts for files, comments, and backlinks. This includes fixing outdated routes (/api/files/upload vs /api/files/upload/:id), verifying metadata/download URLs, verifying thread grouping/reply behavior/resolved_at persistence, and verifying backlinks sync/list semantics.

Fix the frontend upload service contract to match the backend routes exactly. Ensure requestUpload calls /api/files/upload-request, uploadFile handles both presigned PUT uploads and local POST /api/files/upload/:id multipart uploads, fetches/uses final metadata/download URLs correctly, supports progress tracking, and preserves CSRF/session behavior.

Reconcile the global upload tracking/navigation-warning stack so the new upload flows are actually tracked and the app warns users before navigation during active uploads. Align the tracker state, UploadContext, and warning modal behavior with the editor integrations and current app shell mounting.

Integrate the upload service/tracker into the TipTap editor upload flow so uploaded images and attachments become real persisted file URLs in documents. Update the image and file attachment extensions to use the corrected upload service contract, preserve navigation cancellation behavior, and emit persisted URLs that render after reload.

Reconcile the frontend comments data model with the backend thread shape. Fix useCommentsQuery and dependent editor comment code so thread/root/replies/resolved_at are represented consistently, mutations hit the correct endpoints, and the editor can create, reply to, resolve, and render inline comment threads without shape mismatches.

Finish editor/page wiring so the new systems are actually used in the unified document flow. Ensure the editor wrapper/page imports and props propagate upload/comment/backlink behavior into the live document editing experience rather than leaving the services/hooks unused.

Wire backlink syncing from editor content updates so extracted mentions/links are pushed to POST /api/documents/:id/links and existing backlinks can be rendered/queried consistently from document pages. This closes the loop between editor content, document link persistence, and backlinks retrieval.

Update any stale manual verification notes so they reflect the actual required flows from the user request and prior feedback: upload via API, obtain valid URL, embed image in TipTap and confirm rendering, create/reply/resolve a comment thread and confirm resolved_at persistence.

import { useMutation, useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';

# Manual Verification Notes

Use this checklist when validating the new upload, image embedding, and comment lifecycle flows in a local dev or preview environment.

## File upload API
- [ ] Sign in as a normal user.
- [ ] Request `/api/files/upload` from the browser or API client.
- [ ] Confirm the response returns a usable `uploadUrl` plus `fileId` and `s3Key`.
- [ ] If the environment uses local file storage, upload the file to the returned local endpoint.
- [ ] Confirm `GET /api/files/:id` returns a persisted `cdn_url` that is not empty and is usable by the editor.

## TipTap image embedding
- [ ] Open a new document.
- [ ] Insert an image through the editor flow.
- [ ] Confirm the image renders immediately in TipTap.
- [ ] Confirm the temporary preview is replaced by a persisted URL (`http(s)://...` or `/api/files/...`).
- [ ] Reload the page and confirm the image still renders after refresh.

## Comment thread lifecycle
- [ ] Create a root comment on a document.
- [ ] Add a reply using `parent_id`.
- [ ] Verify the thread groups under a single `comment_id` and includes both comments.
- [ ] Resolve the thread.
- [ ] Confirm the resolved comment response includes `resolved_at`.
- [ ] Reload the thread list and confirm `resolved_at` is still present.

## Notes
- The automated coverage for these checks lives in:
  - `e2e/file-upload-api.spec.ts`
  - `e2e/images.spec.ts`
  - `api/src/routes/comments.test.ts`

   166	-- Function and trigger to prevent circular parent references
   167	CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION prevent_circular_parent()
   168	RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   169	DECLARE
   170	  current_parent UUID;
   171	  depth INT := 0;
   172	  max_depth INT := 100;
   173	BEGIN
   174	  IF NEW.parent_id IS NULL THEN
   175	    RETURN NEW;
   176	  END IF;
   177	  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND OLD.parent_id IS NOT DISTINCT FROM NEW.parent_id THEN
   178	    RETURN NEW;
   179	  END IF;
   180	  current_parent := NEW.parent_id;
   181	  WHILE current_parent IS NOT NULL AND depth < max_depth LOOP
   182	    IF current_parent = NEW.id THEN
   183	      RAISE EXCEPTION 'Circular reference detected: document % cannot be a descendant of itself', NEW.id;
   184	    END IF;
   185	    SELECT parent_id INTO current_parent FROM documents WHERE id = current_parent;
   186	    depth := depth + 1;
   187	  END LOOP;
   188	  IF depth >= max_depth THEN
   189	    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Maximum nesting depth (%) exceeded while checking for circular reference', max_depth;
   190	  END IF;
   191	  RETURN NEW;
   192	END;
   193	$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

# Manual Verification Notes

Use this checklist when validating the new upload, image embedding, and comment lifecycle flows in a local dev or preview environment.

## File upload API
- [ ] Sign in as a normal user.
- [ ] Request `/api/files/upload` from the browser or API client.
- [ ] Confirm the response returns a usable `uploadUrl` plus `fileId` and `s3Key`.
- [ ] If the environment uses local file storage, upload the file to the returned local endpoint.
- [ ] Confirm `GET /api/files/:id` returns a persisted `cdn_url` that is not empty and is usable by the editor.

## TipTap image embedding
- [ ] Open a new document.
- [ ] Insert an image through the editor flow.
- [ ] Confirm the image renders immediately in TipTap.
- [ ] Confirm the temporary preview is replaced by a persisted URL (`http(s)://...` or `/api/files/...`).
- [ ] Reload the page and confirm the image still renders after refresh.

## Comment thread lifecycle
- [ ] Create a root comment on a document.
- [ ] Add a reply using `parent_id`.
- [ ] Verify the thread groups under a single `comment_id` and includes both comments.
- [ ] Resolve the thread.
- [ ] Confirm the resolved comment response includes `resolved_at`.
- [ ] Reload the thread list and confirm `resolved_at` is still present.

## Notes
- The automated coverage for these checks lives in:
  - `e2e/file-upload-api.spec.ts`
  - `e2e/images.spec.ts`
  - `api/src/routes/comments.test.ts`

messages.content:
Implement file upload and inline comments systems for Ship.

For file uploads, create `routes/files.ts` with auth middleware: POST /api/files/upload-request (validates filename/mimeType/sizeBytes with Zod, blocks dangerous extensions like .exe/.bat/.sh/.dll/.jar/.ps1, creates a files row with status=pending, returns a presigned S3 upload URL if S3_UPLOADS_BUCKET is configured or a local upload endpoint for dev), POST /api/files/upload/:id (for local dev: accepts multipart form data, writes to uploads/ directory, updates files row to status=uploaded), GET /api/files/:id (returns file metadata + download URL). For S3: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 and @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner with 15-minute URL expiration. For local dev: serve uploads/ as static files. Set max file size to 1GB. Validate UUID format on all ID params to prevent path traversal.

For comments, create `routes/comments.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/comments (list all comment threads for a document, grouped by comment_id), POST /api/documents/:id/comments (create a new comment thread with comment_id matching the TipTap comment mark, or reply to existing via parent_id), PATCH /api/comments/:id (update comment content), POST /api/comments/:id/resolve (set resolved_at timestamp), DELETE /api/comments/:id (only author or admin can delete). Create `routes/backlinks.ts` with: GET /api/documents/:id/backlinks (query document_links where target_id matches, return source documents with title and type), POST /api/documents/:id/links (upsert document_links from extracted mentions/links in content).

On the frontend, create `src/services/upload.ts` with: requestUpload (calls upload-request endpoint), uploadFile (either uploads directly to presigned S3 URL or to the local endpoint), trackUploadProgress. Create `src/services/uploadTracker.ts` to track active uploads and warn on navigation (UploadNavigationWarning component). Create `src/hooks/useCommentsQuery.ts` for fetching and mutating comments via TanStack Query.

Verify: upload an image via the API, confirm it returns a valid URL, embed it in a TipTap document via the editor, confirm it renders. Create a comment thread on a document, reply to it, resolve it, confirm the resolved_at timestamp is set.
